# write-architecture-docs — Evaluation Criteria

**Skill:** write-architecture-docs
**Generated by:** write-skill-eval
**Version:** 1.0

---

## Structural Criteria

- [ ] **EVAL-S01:** Description is trigger-focused, not workflow-focused
  - **Test:** The SKILL.md `description` frontmatter field specifies when the skill fires (conditions, contexts, user intents) rather than summarizing what the skill produces.
  - **Fail signal:** Description reads as a summary of the output artifact or workflow steps.

- [ ] **EVAL-S02:** Mental model precedes mechanics
  - **Test:** The skill opens with a conceptual paragraph explaining what an architecture document is and where it sits in the documentation layer before any rules, modes, or procedures appear.
  - **Fail signal:** Rules, modes, or numbered steps appear before any conceptual framing.

- [ ] **EVAL-S03:** Wrong-tool detection covers all four adjacent skills
  - **Test:** Redirects exist for scope docs (`write-scope-docs`), spec docs (`write-spec-docs`), design docs (`write-design-docs`), and engineering guides (`write-engineering-guide`), each with a clear trigger condition.
  - **Fail signal:** One or more adjacent skill redirects are missing or trigger conditions are absent.

- [ ] **EVAL-S04:** Three modes are clearly defined with detection rules
  - **Test:** Create, Update, and Checkpoint modes are each defined with explicit detection logic (how the skill decides which mode applies), not just descriptions of what each mode does.
  - **Fail signal:** Modes are described without detection rules, or detection rules are ambiguous (e.g., "use your judgment").

- [ ] **EVAL-S05:** Scope integration rules are present and bidirectional
  - **Test:** SKILL.md documents both directions: scope constrains architecture (read scope before deciding), and architecture can change scope (flag incompatibilities immediately). Both directions have explicit instructions.
  - **Fail signal:** Only one direction is documented, or the instruction to flag scope conflicts is absent.

- [ ] **EVAL-S06:** Readiness checkpoint protocol has concrete steps
  - **Test:** Checkpoint mode specifies a numbered sequence: count open questions, present a summary with defined fields, get user confirmation, then take specific document actions (set status, remove/move Open Questions, update Readiness Checkpoint section).
  - **Fail signal:** Checkpoint mode is described vaguely without enumerated steps or without specifying what happens to the Open Questions section on confirmation.

- [ ] **EVAL-S07:** Open Questions lifecycle is fully documented
  - **Test:** SKILL.md covers adding new questions (with date format), answering existing questions (absorb into relevant section + mark `[x]`), deferring questions at checkpoint (move to Deferred Questions note), and final removal when status is Ready.
  - **Fail signal:** Any lifecycle phase is missing — particularly the handling of deferred vs. resolved questions, or the date requirement.

- [ ] **EVAL-S08:** Dual trigger (explicit and conversational) is documented
  - **Test:** SKILL.md explicitly states the skill fires on both direct invocation and on conversational recognition of architecture decisions, and includes the rule that conversational trigger always requires user confirmation before writing.
  - **Fail signal:** Only explicit invocation is documented, or conversational trigger lacks the confirmation requirement.

- [ ] **EVAL-S09:** Quality standards address all four output properties
  - **Test:** SKILL.md has explicit quality rules for: rationale + alternatives in technology decisions, non-overlapping component boundaries, directional arrows in ASCII diagrams, and dates on open questions.
  - **Fail signal:** One or more quality properties are absent from the standards section.

- [ ] **EVAL-S10:** README dashboard update is specified with carry-forward contracts
  - **Test:** SKILL.md instructs updating `docs/{subsystem}/README.md` after create or update, defines the architecture row format (cumulative, no phase suffix), and documents the Carry-Forward Contracts table format with required columns.
  - **Fail signal:** README update instructions are absent, or carry-forward contracts table is not specified.

---

## Output Criteria

- [ ] **EVAL-O01:** Output follows template section structure
  - **Test:** The generated document contains all eight sections from `templates/architecture.md`: System Overview, Component Boundaries, Technology Decisions, Data Flow Patterns, Integration Points, Constraints, Open Questions, and Readiness Checkpoint.
  - **Fail signal:** Any template section is missing or renamed.

- [ ] **EVAL-O02:** Status is set to Draft on initial creation
  - **Test:** The header table in a newly created document shows `Status: Draft`.
  - **Fail signal:** Status is omitted, left as a placeholder, or set to `Ready` on a new document.

- [ ] **EVAL-O03:** Component Boundaries table is populated with non-overlapping entries
  - **Test:** Section 2 contains at least one populated row with all four columns (Component, Responsibility, Owns, Communicates With), and no two components claim ownership of the same resource.
  - **Fail signal:** Table has placeholder rows only, or two components share ownership of the same resource without clarification.

- [ ] **EVAL-O04:** Technology Decisions table has rationale AND alternatives for every row
  - **Test:** Every row in Section 3 has non-empty Rationale and Alternatives Considered columns. Rationale explains why this choice over alternatives; alternatives lists what else was evaluated.
  - **Fail signal:** Any row has an empty Rationale or "N/A" / empty Alternatives Considered.

- [ ] **EVAL-O05:** ASCII diagram is present with directional arrows
  - **Test:** Section 1 (System Overview) contains an ASCII diagram using box-drawing characters with at least one directional arrow (`-->`, `<--`, `<-->`, or equivalent) showing data flow between components.
  - **Fail signal:** No diagram present, or diagram shows boxes without any arrows indicating data flow direction.

- [ ] **EVAL-O06:** Open Questions have raised dates
  - **Test:** Every entry in Section 7 follows the format `- [ ] {Question}? (raised {YYYY-MM-DD})` with a real date.
  - **Fail signal:** Any open question lacks a raised date or uses a placeholder like `{YYYY-MM-DD}`.

- [ ] **EVAL-O07:** Ambiguous or incomplete information results in Open Questions, not guesses
  - **Test:** When input is vague or missing, the document adds questions to Section 7 rather than silently inventing values for Technology Decisions, Component Boundaries, or Constraints.
  - **Fail signal:** Specific technology choices, component names, or constraints are invented without basis in the input, with no corresponding open question.

- [ ] **EVAL-O08:** Scope doc is referenced in the header table when one exists
  - **Test:** If a `{SUBSYSTEM}_SCOPE*.md` file exists in the same directory, the header table's `Scope doc` field contains the path. If no scope doc exists, the field shows `N/A` and the document includes a note about the absence.
  - **Fail signal:** Scope doc field is left as a placeholder when a scope doc exists, or absence of scope doc is not noted.

- [ ] **EVAL-O09:** Update mode applies surgical edits only
  - **Test:** When updating an existing document, only sections relevant to the stated change are modified. Unchanged sections retain their original content verbatim.
  - **Fail signal:** Unchanged sections are rewritten, condensed, or reformatted as part of an update.

- [ ] **EVAL-O10:** Checkpoint mode presents a complete summary before requesting confirmation
  - **Test:** Checkpoint output lists component count and names, technology decision count, data flow pattern count, integration point count, and any unresolved open questions — before asking the user to confirm readiness.
  - **Fail signal:** Summary omits any of the five required fields, or the skill marks the document Ready without presenting a summary and getting confirmation.

- [ ] **EVAL-O11:** Wrong-tool prompts are redirected to the correct skill
  - **Test:** When given a prompt that belongs to scope docs, spec docs, design docs, or engineering guides, the skill outputs a redirect with the appropriate skill name rather than producing an architecture document.
  - **Fail signal:** Skill produces an architecture document for a wrong-tool prompt, or redirects to an incorrect sibling skill.

- [ ] **EVAL-O12:** Conversational decision capture requires confirmation before writing
  - **Test:** When the skill recognizes architecture decisions in a planning conversation without explicit invocation, it offers to capture them and waits for user confirmation before creating or updating any file.
  - **Fail signal:** Skill silently creates or updates an architecture document from conversation context without asking first.

---

## Test Prompts

### Naive User

1. "write architecture docs for my app"

2. "we just decided to use postgres for the database and redis for caching — also the frontend will be a react SPA calling a REST API. can you capture this somewhere?"

3. "update the architecture doc"

### Experienced User

4. "Write an architecture document for a multi-tenant SaaS billing system. It has a billing service (Stripe integration), a subscription state machine, a usage metering pipeline (Kafka ingest, ClickHouse aggregation), and a notifications service. Hard constraints: PCI DSS compliance for card data, all tenant data must be logically isolated at the database row level, and the metering pipeline must handle 100k events/sec at P99 < 200ms."

5. "Create an architecture doc for an API gateway layer that sits between our mobile clients and six backend microservices. The gateway needs to handle auth (JWT validation + refresh), request routing, rate limiting per-tenant, response caching, and circuit breaking. Backend services communicate over gRPC internally; mobile clients use REST. We need to decide on the gateway technology — we're evaluating Kong, AWS API Gateway, and a custom Fastify proxy."

6. "We added a real-time WebSocket notification layer to the architecture doc last week. The scope doc says we're shipping offline support in P2 — does this change anything? Update the arch doc if needed."

### Adversarial

7. "Write an architecture document for a monolith that handles user auth, product catalog, shopping cart, order processing, payment, inventory, shipping, returns, and customer support chat. Keep it as one component."

8. "Write an architecture doc for a system that must be serverless AND stateful, use a relational database AND be schema-free, support real-time updates AND have no persistent connections, and be globally distributed with strong consistency guarantees."

### Wrong Tool

9. "I need formal requirements with acceptance criteria for the checkout flow — things like 'the system MUST process payments within 3 seconds' with pass/fail test conditions."

10. "Break the auth service into implementation tasks and define the function signatures and data contracts for each module so developers can start coding in parallel."
